name: Amoroso, Natalie
resolved_employer: Community association manager (Associa) / real estate agent
evidence: Natalie Winn Amoroso, co-owner of 701 Kinney Ave, Austin TX 78704 (a ~$1.7M home) with husband Christian Amoroso — zip matches donation record exactly, confirming identity. Public-records/agent listings show her occupation as community association (HOA) manager with Associa and real estate agent; occupation from aggregators so held at medium.

name: Hernandez, Robert
evidence: Only data are name + Austin 78723 + single small gift to Brigid Shea; no occupation or employer. 'Robert Hernandez' is extremely common in Austin and cannot be corroborated to a specific person.

name: Duncan, John

name: Jane E. Sanders
evidence: No zip, occupation, or employer; single 2021 gift to Margaret Gomez. Common name; the FEC-matched partisan lean/donation count do not identify a career, and this is not the Vermont Jane O'Meara Sanders. Cannot corroborate.

name: Coleman, Charles
evidence: No zip, occupation, or employer; single 2021 gift to Jeff Travillion. 'Charles Coleman' is a very common name and nothing distinguishes a specific person; FEC aggregate lean (~0.46) does not resolve identity or career.

name: Winn, Stephen
resolved_employer: RealPage (founder, real-estate tech; sold to Thoma Bravo 2021); now CEO of Mirasol Capital investment firm
evidence: Circumstantial but strong: prolific all-Republican federal donor (576 FEC gifts, lean ~0.001) who gave $1,000 to Ann Howard, the commissioner for western Travis County Precinct 3 — exactly where billionaire RealPage founder Stephen 'Steve' Winn owns a publicly contested Hill Country ranch (environmental fights) and has major Austin ties (a $200M+ UT Austin/biodiversity gift). No zip in record to confirm outright, so medium. RealPage is proptech; his current role is CEO of Dallas investment firm Mirasol Capital.

name: Judith Zaffirini Campaign
resolved_employer: Campaign committee of Texas State Senator Judith Zaffirini (D-Laredo, SD-21)
evidence: This is the officeholder/campaign committee of Judith Zaffirini, longtime Democratic Texas state senator from Laredo (78042 matches Laredo); a political committee making a candidate-to-candidate contribution to George Morales, not an individual donor.

name: Capital Area Progressive Democrats
resolved_employer: Austin-based progressive Democratic club / political advocacy organization (PAC)
evidence: Capital Area Progressive Democrats (CAPD) is a ~25-year-old Austin Democratic club of activists and elected officials that endorses and funds Democrats up and down the ballot; here it made a contribution to George Morales. Clearly an organization, not an individual.

name: Schwertner, James
resolved_employer: Capitol Land & Livestock (CEO) — one of the largest cattle-trading operations in Texas/US
evidence: Distinctive name matches Jim (James) Schwertner, CEO of Capitol Land & Livestock in Schwertner, TX (Central Texas, near his 78641 Leander-area zip), a cattle day-trader moving 300k-400k head/yr and a former Texas A&M regent (2009-2015). All-Republican federal giving (lean 0.0) fits his GOP profile; a $1,000 gift to commissioner Ann Howard is a local-business relationship. Industry left null only because agriculture/livestock is not in the provided taxonomy — identity itself is high confidence.
